reasoning: The FRED statistical tool gives a strong 68% prior based on recent upward momentum (4 consecutive days up) and historical seasonal patterns (it increased around this exact time last year). Over a very short 1-week horizon, currencies generally follow a random walk (near 50%), but short-term momentum and month-end/quarter-end rebalancing flows often show slight persistence over a few days. Wide macro trends point to a broadly weakening dollar (DXY down to ~97-100 recently), but in the very near term (one week), the FRED tool's recent daily momentum estimate is the most applicable signal. Balancing the 50% random-walk baseline against the 68% statistical indicator yields a forecast of 67%.
